title: IPMC SARS-CoV-2 Two-Step qPCR Protocol on BIOMARK
author: Julien Fassy
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bd3ii8ke

steps.instruction: To be performed in the appropriate biosafety conditions (BSL2 laboratory) Transfer the totality of the transport medium into a 2 mL cryotube. OPTIONAL : in the case of a non-virucide transport medium, heat the sample for 10 min at 65°C. This step should strongly decrease the virus infectivity but might not end up in total inactivation. The effect of heat inactivation may lead to a decrease of test sensitivity depending on the type of swab transport medium. Manufacturer's instructions in the attached file. HB-0354-007_HB_QA_Viral_RNA_Mini_0720_WW.pdf OPTIONAL: Addition of carrier RNA (provided in the kit) to Buffer AVL to improve yield. Follow the table provided in the handbook to prepare a solution containing 5.6 μg of carrier RNA per sample. We have compared protocols containing or not this step and did not find any significant differences regarding the sensitivity of detection. Transfer 140 µL sample into a 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tube.
Add 560 Buffer AVL. Close the tube caps and vortex for >15 s. Note: If using a volume of sample other than 140 µL, increase the amount of Buffer AVL proportionally.
Briefly centrifuge the tube to remove drops from the inside of the lid
Go to Qiacube. Elution volume: default 60 µL See Qiacube protocol in the attached file. Virus_QIAampViralRNA_BodyFluid_ManualLysis_V2.pdf
Thaw all reagents on ice. Briefly vortex and centrifuge the reagents before using. Manufacturer's instructions in the attached file. cDNA-Prep-RT-MM_qr_100-6472.pdf

In a PCR plate (on ice), prepare a pre-mix of the Reverse Transcription Master Mix and water as indicated in the following table. %s %s Component Volume per Reaction (μL) Volume for 48 Reactions* (μL) Volume for 96 Reactions* (μL) Volume for 192 Reactions* (μL) PRE-MIX Reverse Transcription Master Mix 1.0 52.8 105.6 211.2 (Fluidigm PN 100-6297) RNase-free water 2.0 105.6 211.2 422.4 * Includes 10% overage for ease of pipetting.
Add 2 µL of RNA to each well containing pre-mix, to reach a total volume of 5 μL.
Properly seal and gently vortex to mix the reverse transcription reactions.
Centrifuge the reactions 1000 rpm, Room temperature, 1 minute
Place in a standard thermal cycler. Incubate using the following protocol: %s %s
Prepare Pre-Mix and Samples: In a DNA-free hood, prepare the sample pre-mix for the reactions as shown in the following table: %s %s * Includes 10% overage.
In a PCR plate, aliquot 2.25 μL of pre-mix for each sample.
Remove the plate from the DNA-free hood and add 2.75 μL of cDNA to each well containing pre-mix, making a total volume of 5 μL.
Mix the reactions by briefly vortexing, and then centrifuge 1000 rpm, Room temperature, 1 minute
Place the plate in the thermal cycler and cycle using the following table as a guide: %s %s
After cycling, dilute the reaction 1:5 by adding 20 μL Dilution Reagent (Fluidigm PN 100-8726) to the final 5 μL reaction volume for a total volume of 25 μL.

Manufacturer's instructions for general 192.24 IFC protocol 192.24_GE_TaqMan-Std_qr_100-6170.pdf Prepare the IFC Inject control line fluid into accumulator 2 (Acc2) on the IFC. Manufacturer's intruction for control line fluid loading Control+Line+Fluid_Loading_qr_68000132r07.pdf
Remove and discard the blue protective film from the bottom of the IFC.
Prepare 10X Assays In a DNA-free hood, prepare aliquots of 10X assays using volumes in the following table. Scale up appropriately for multiple runs. %s %s
Prepare Sample Pre-Mix and Samples Combine components in the following table to make sample pre-mix and final sample mixture. Scale up appropriately for multiple runs. %s %s

In a DNA-free hood, combine the TaqMan Universal PCR Master Mix with the GE Sample Loading Reagent in a 1.5 mL sterile tube—enough volume to fill an entire IFC. 2.2 μL of this sample pre-mix can then be aliquoted for each sample.
Remove these aliquots from the DNA-free hood and add 1.8 μL of cDNA to each, making a total volume of 4 μL in each aliquot.
Pipet 3 μL of each assay and 3 μL of each sample into the respective inlets on the IFC (see the 192.24 IFC pipetting map).
lncubate for 10 min Room temperature
Prime IFC on the Controller. Place the IFC into the instrument and run the load script: - Juno:Load Mix 192.24 GE - RX: Load Mix (169x) IMPORTANT : Start IFC run within 1 hour of loading samples.
Pipet 150 μL of Actuation Fluid into the P1 well on the IFC.
Pipet 150 μL of Pressure Fluid into the P2 and P3 wells on the IFC.
Pipet 20 μL of Pressure Fluid into the P4 and P5 wells on the IFC.
Blot the carrier surface with a dry, lint-free cloth.
Remove any dust particles or debris from the IFC surface with clear tape.
Start data collection on Biomark HD Biomark HD (Fluidigm, BMKHD-BMKHD)
Double-click the Data Collection icon on the desktop.
Click Start a New Run. Ensure that the status indicators for the lamp and the camera are green.
Place the loaded IFC into the Biomark HD.
Choose project settings (if applicable). Click Next.
Click Load.
Verify IFC barcode and IFC type.
Choose project settings (if applicable). Click Next.
Provide a name and select a file storage location for a new IFC run, or browse to select a predefined run file. Click Next.

Choose the application, reference, and probes: a) Application type: Gene Expression b) Passive reference: ROX c) Assay: Single probe d) Probe type: if using a dark quencher choose FAM-Non fluo (Need to be added manually into the Biomark) or FAM-MGB. e) Click Next.
Browse to and choose the thermal protocol: below the thermal protocol (Taqman Covid) we have used:
Confirm Auto Exposure is selected. Click Next.
Verify IFC run information
Click Start Run
Extracted samples can be stored at -20 °C (or -80 °C) for further processing.
Tubes can be thouroughly wiped with PHAGO’SPRAY ND (Phagogene) or Virospray (Sterisciences) and taken out the BSL2 laboratory.

Pool primer and probes with 2 steps: - Intermediate solution : mix together primers and probe for each target gene (substep 14.1) - Pooled Taqman assay mix : pool the intermediate solution into a final pooled assay (substep 14.2)
Prepare an intermediate solution by mixing primers and probe for each target, with the following volumes: %s %s

Pool each intermediate solution into a pooled Taqman assay mix (0,2X) using TE (10 mM Tris-HCl, pH 8.0, 0.1 mM EDTA) so that each primer is at a final concentration of 180 nM, and each probe at 50 nM. This step should be adapted depending on the number of primers/probes to be used in the final pooled assay (ideally 8 or 12 sets of primers for a 192.24 IFC). The chart below provides an example using 4 assays used to detect SARS-CoV-2 %s %s Note: Volume can be adjusted proportionally based on the number of samples to be amplified GE_Preamp-MM_TaqMan_qr_100-5876C2.pdf
